introMarkdown:
During the period from April 27, 2026, to May 4, 2026, the team shipped a total of 11 releases across five repositories, indicating a steady deployment frequency.

The docker-base-images repository emerged as the busiest project, contributing five of the total releases. The most recent release, docker-base-images 0.4.0, was made available on May 4, 2026. Additionally, the ci-github-publish repository saw two releases, while ci-github-container and terraform-modules each had one release. These updates reflect ongoing enhancements in container management and infrastructure as code capabilities, which are critical for effective deployment and resource management.

- The release of docker-base-images 0.4.0 is significant for platform engineers as it may include optimizations that improve image build times and reduce resource consumption.
- The updates in ci-github-publish and ci-github-container enhance CI/CD workflows, providing SREs with more robust tools for automating deployment processes.
- The release from terraform-modules introduces new infrastructure capabilities that can simplify the provisioning of cloud resources, which is essential for technical contributors managing complex environments.
closingMarkdown:
Period: April 27, 2026 - May 4, 2026
Repositories covered: 5
Releases shipped: 11
Busiest project: docker-base-images (5)
Most recent release: docker-base-images 0.4.0

Repositories to note:
- ci-github-publish (2 releases, 5 ⭐)
- ci-github-container (1 release, 5 ⭐)
- terraform-modules (1 release, 3 ⭐)

During this period, significant activity occurred across multiple repositories, particularly with the docker-base-images project, which accounted for half of the total releases. The recent updates in the ci-github-publish and ci-github-container repositories may also provide valuable enhancements for users focused on CI/CD workflows.

For platform engineers, SREs, and other technical users, these updates present opportunities for improved performance and automation in their workflows.

Next steps:
- Review the documentation for the latest docker-base-images 0.4.0 release to identify any changes that may enhance container performance.
- Run benchmarks using the ci-github-container to assess the impact of the latest release on CI/CD pipeline efficiency.
- Explore the terraform-modules repository for potential compliance improvements in infrastructure as code practices.

repositories.name: ci-github-container
repositories.description: Opinionated GitHub Actions and workflows for continuous integration in container (OCI) context

highlights:
## Release Summary

Documentation for actions and workflows has been updated.

Internal changes include GitHub Actions dependency updates across multiple directories and an npm `yaml` dependency bump for the Helm release chart action.

repositories.name: ci-github-nodejs
repositories.description: Opinionated Github actions and workflows for continuous integration in NodeJs context

highlights:
## Release Summary

Documentation for actions and workflows was updated.

Internal maintenance updated GitHub Actions dependencies across multiple directories.

## Breaking change(s)

There is no breaking change.

## Release Summary

Documentation for actions and workflows has been updated to improve user guidance.

Internal maintenance updates bumped the devcontainer Node feature and refreshed GitHub Actions dependencies across the project.

## Breaking change(s)

There is no breaking change.

repositories.name: ci-github-publish
repositories.description: Opinionated GitHub actions and workflows for streamlined release, deployment, and publishing.

highlights:
## Release Summary

Actions and workflows documentation was updated to reflect current usage.

Internal GitHub Actions dependencies were bumped.

## Breaking change(s)

There is no breaking change.

## Release Summary

Documentation for actions and workflows was updated.

Permissions updates, these workflows does not require `id-token: write` anymore.

- .github/workflows/release.yml
- .github/workflows/prepare-release.yml
- .github/workflows/finish-deploy-argocd-app-of-apps.yml
- .github/workflows/deploy-start.yml
- .github/workflows/deploy-finish.yml
- .github/workflows/deploy-checks.yml
- .github/workflows/deploy-argocd-app-of-apps.yml
- .github/workflows/clean-deploy.yml
- .github/workflows/clean-deploy-argocd-app-of-apps.yml

Internal changes include GitHub Actions dependency updates across multiple directories.

## Breaking change(s)

There is no breaking change.

## Release Summary

The reusable CI workflow now uses the published `testcontainers-node` image with shared-CI local tag handoff for more consistent image reuse.
For `argocd-cmp-hk-deployment-upgrade-deps`, dependencies were upgraded, including Argo CD patch updates up to `v3.3.9`.
The release process now correctly handles the `prerelease` input.

Internal updates include refreshed actions/workflows documentation and dependency maintenance for GitHub Actions, test `nginx`, and build tooling.
